title: Level 624
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'IM Fell English'; } }} {{Class 4}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 624: "Yet Another Dangerous Abandoned Level"'''}}{{Blurbackground|file=624-1-1.png}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=624-1-1.png|caption=The first image taken of '''Level 624'''.}} '''Level 624''' takes the appearance of an infinitely expanding shopping mall with diverse amounts of storefronts and shops. The entirety of '''Level 624''' appears to be abandoned in nature, with an abstract amount of corrosion present in specific areas, though most of '''Level 624''' appears to be sterile. Many lights within '''Level 624''' are either busted or switched off completely, resulting in large areas of the level being shrouded in a perpetual darkness. In terms of temperature, '''Level 624''' typically lingers at a cool 58 degrees Fahrenheit (14.4 degrees Celsius) though this metric can increase substantially within lit areas. '''Level 624's''' architectural layout appears to shift around at random intervals, with hallways being rerouted to different places, larger hallways shrinking into smaller ones, staircases becoming elevators, and shopfronts being relocated to different areas of the level. This phenomenon does not affect the whole of '''Level 624''', but rather specific areas at one time. As well as architectural changes, the appearance of specific areas within '''Level 624''' can change as well, resulting in different styles and levels of corrosion throughout the level. It is recommended to leave the area or seek shelter during a shift, as they risk dangers such as suffocating within spontaneously generated pillars and falling through holes that were formerly a connecting bridge or hallway. Each change takes varying amounts of time to complete depending on the size of the affected area, ranging from 5 minutes to 16 days. Shopfronts provide the only modals of escape from '''Level 624's''' architectural shifts, as they are only moved to different positions, though shopfronts are still prone to the level's style changes. Each shopfront varies in appearance, from small stands to large department stores, and may contain staff only rooms or restrooms depending on the size of the store. Products can be rarely found on shelving, though they are always expired. Products within stores change during appearance shifts and can be replaced altogether during a major change. Wandering far enough into '''Level 624''' reveals windows and doors seemingly showing the "outside" of the level, though it is inaccessible due to both the door and the window's indestructible nature. The "outside" is a large parking lot in a depressing state of corrosion, with a large number of cracks and worn markings. Trees border the lot in the far end, and an everlasting thunderstorm rages above. Despite the typical nature of windows, one is able to feel aspects of the outside, such as wind and rain, simply by standing near a window or door. During specific periods, areas of '''Level 624''' are capable of being flooded with water on the lowest level, typically not passing knee height. The water retains a murky hue and remains completely still even when one enters. Though the water appears harmless, it usually harbors hazards such as sharp debris, live electrical wires, and an assortment of microbes and bacteria, most commonly the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. As a result, it is unrecommended to enter a pool of water unless absolutely necessary, and if one sees the water level beginning to rise, it is advised to leave the area immediately.
Recovered Document
'''I've seen better...'''

{{Blurimage|caption='''What I am so used to seeing.'''|file=624-1.png}} As I entered the space, I was greeted by a large atrium arranged in a circular formation, above a wide hallway bordered by small hole in the wall shops. Two hallways diverged from this courtyard, connecting to more hallways. I took one hallway and began exploring. The hallways lead to more hallways, which lead to more areas. Corrosion was everywhere within the level, as well as pools of water on the lower levels, imminent of the level's abandoned nature. This level felt unfamiliar to me in the scale of the Backrooms; it did not fit any level I have ever come across in the [[The Database|Database]]. As I dwelled on that prospect further, I concluded that I have discovered a new level. I should have experienced the typical feelings that come from discovering a new level; maybe uncertainty and fear about not knowing any exits or ways to survive, maybe a stinge of curiosity about wanting to unearth more of the level's properties. Or maybe a sense of excitement, as you have finally saw the unknown and left a mark on the history of knowledge within the Backrooms. But I did not feel any of these, instead I felt... ''bored''. Sure, there was the constantly shifting layout, the anomalous windows and doors, and the random objects scattered about. However, there was nothing special about the space. The shifting changes was basically non-Euclidean geometry but glorified and more predictable. The indestructible windows and doors were a property that could always be found within many levels. Despite all of those, the most glaring aspect of the level was the fact that it was set in a large mall, heavily corroded in various areas. There was no unique property to make the level stand out, it was just simply a large mall with abnormal properties that can be found basically anywhere in the Backrooms. Since the level was abandoned, I automatically figured there were entities, and, sure enough, I survived 16 different entity encounters while on the level. I remember when I would enter a new space in the Backrooms during my first days of being here. Initially, you feel fear, but after becoming accustomed to the nature of the Backrooms, you feel a sense of adventure, a desire to know more about what each level has to offer. But, eventually, that spark dies down, and you feel like you have already known it all. Every space is similar in some way and fashion, and they share a lot of similar properties. And this level is a representation of that, as there is nothing truly "new" to find within the Backrooms anymore. '''Level 624''', like many, is just yet another dangerous abandoned level, and it will always remain that way.
==Entities== Many entities inhabit '''Level 624''', such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 44|Silver Slimes]], and [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], though these entities settle in specific areas of the level and change positions during the level's architectural shifts, often vanishing and appearing instantaneously as the level changes. This makes '''Level 624''' even more challenging, as one would have to constantly be on alert in case of a entity suddenly spawning nearby. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Entering a shopping mall on any outdoor level will bring you here. ===Exits=== *Entering a store with the 7/11 logo on it brings one to [[Level 711]] or [[Level 114]]. *Noclipping through a window has a chance to bring one to the outside of [[Level 156]]. *Submerging one's head in a pool of water for extended periods of time will bring one to [[Level 298]]. *Jumping from at least the second story of the level has a chance to bring one to [[Level 694]]. *Doors in pink rooms send one to [[Level 479]].

'''Author:''' [[User:SnowyDasher47|Snow]]
Another shorter page. This level is a representation of a large number of levels being similar and a bit bland, as well as your average "abandoned" level. I hope you all had a nice read. {{User:SnowyDasher47/SnowyDasherPages}}
